headlines.description: Bernardo Silva scored an early goal but Erling Haaland missed a penalty as struggling Manchester City drew 1-1 with Everton to kick off a full slate of Boxing Day games on Thursday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Haaland misses penalty as City held by Everton

headlines.description: Bournemouth slipped to sixth in the Premier League as they played out a 0-0 home draw with Crystal Palace on Thursday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Bournemouth held to goalless draw by Palace

headlines.description: Fulham came from behind to beat neighbours Chelsea 2-1 after a 95th minute goal from substitute Rodrigo Muniz gave the visitors all three point in the Premier League on Thursday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Fulham stun Chelsea with last-gasp Muniz winner

headlines.description: Newcastle United beat Aston Villa 3-0 in the Premier League on Thursday with goals from Anthony Gordon, Alexander Isak and Joelinton while Jhon Duran was sent off for the visitors who crashed to a fifth straight away defeat.
headlines.shortLinkText: Newcastle thump 10-man Aston Villa to go 5th

headlines.description: Nottingham Forest continued charting their climb up the Premier League table after they beat Tottenham Hotspur 1-0 on Thursday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Elanga strikes for Forest as limp Spurs lose again

headlines.description: Jarrod Bowen scored a second half winner for West Ham United in a 1-0 Premier League victory at Southampton as new Saints manager Ivan Juric suffered defeat in his first game in charge of the struggling club on Thursday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Bowen's Hammer blow downs Saints in Juric bow

headlines.description: Manchester United captain Bruno Fernandes was sent off as his side went down 2-0 at Wolverhampton Wanderers on Thursday -- a fourth defeat in their last five Premier League games.
headlines.shortLinkText: Bruno sees red as Wolves pile misery on United

headlines.description: Liverpool came back after conceding early to beat Leicester City 3-1 at Anfield on Thursday and open a seven-point lead at the top of the Premier League.
headlines.shortLinkText: Liverpool go seven points clear after Leicester win
